02 Installing Your Scalable OS - YouTube.mp4
There are three parts to installing any company operating system. Part one, we simply need to finish building out your operating system dashboard. So this is the... tool, the software that you have been working with. And there's a good chance at this point that you probably have some documents and some notes, you know, in a Google drive somewhere, or, you know, maybe even written down on a post-it note. You just need to capture all that and go ahead and get it in one place in your operating system dashboard. So that's part one. Part two is we need to... host your first quarterly sprint planning meeting. Now, this is a two-day meeting. Day one is that clarity day that we've talked about on the clarity. day.
This is when you're going to work with your team and you're going to finalize your clarity compass. Once you've done that, that's great. You're not going to need to repeat that again for another three years. Day two is when you're going to hold your first quarterly sprint planning meeting. So now that we know what our three-year target is and we have our 12Q planning grid, it becomes pretty simple to say, 'all right. Let's build a plan for this first 90-day quarterly sprint. So this needs to happen, right? And this is when you're actually installing the operating system. Initially, with your leadership team, because they're going to have the clarity compass, and you will have executed your first quarterly sprint planning meeting. Now, once that is done.
Part three, you need to hold an all-hands rollout meeting. This is when your operating system is officially installed, not just at the leadership level, but throughout the entire organization. Now, we're going to include a separate training on how to hold and facilitate this rollout meeting, but the basic idea is that you're going to formally... announce your clarity compass, right? You're going to share with your team. This is our three-year target. This is our company purpose. These are our values and these are our strategic anchors. What you're also going to do is roll out your very first quarterly sprint plan and get everyone excited about the next quarter that is to come. Once you've held your first quarterly sprint planning meeting and announced your three-year target, company purpose, core values, and strategic anchors to everyone in your organization, your operating system is officially installed. But remember, the work of maintaining and upgrading your company OS is never truly complete. And that's exactly what I want to leave you with.
